meaning: n.切分法,省略中间的音节,中略
examples: The master of syncopation in music was Scott Joplin, African American composer writing a lot around the area of St. Louis at the turn of the twentieth century.
Okay. So that's what syncopation is and it isn't much more difficult than that.
Above his dining table, a 1976 Jasper Johns silk-screen in blues, yellows and reds jumps with syncopation.
In the late 1890s, syncopation joined with soulful melodies, upbeat dance tunes united with the sultry sound of brass instruments, and jazz began to emerge.
